title: Data & Strategy Analyst, Real Estate
description:
About Cerberus




Founded in 1992, Cerberus is a global leader in alternative investing with approximately $70 billion in assets across complementary credit, private equity, and real estate strategies. We invest across the capital structure where our integrated investment platforms and proprietary operating capabilities create an edge to improve performance and drive long-term value. Our tenured teams have experience working collaboratively across asset classes, sectors, and geographies to seek strong risk-adjusted returns for our investors. For more information about our people and platforms, visit us at www.cerberus.com.




Job Purpose:

Cerberus established our Residential Opportunities investment platform in 2008 to capitalize on opportunities to acquire distressed residential mortgage-backed securities following the Global Financial Crisis. Over the past decade, our Residential Opportunities platform has continued to build upon its expertise in residential debt and evolved to earn recognition as an innovator and market leader in securitizing performing residential real estate loans and investing in single-family rental real estate (SFRs).

Our Residential Opportunities strategies seek to maximize absolute return through a combination of capital appreciation and current income, investing primarily in mortgages, loans, and real assets, and leveraging our proprietary asset management platforms to enhance performance and drive value.




As an Analyst at Cerberus Operations and Advisory, you will help the team expand its analytic and modeling capabilities across all aspects of Cerberus’ Residential Real Estate strategy, from analyzing hard asset and capital markets transactions to supporting operational excellence. Using the team’s tailored data, you will be expected to create reports, model asset performance, and support research projects. You will partner with portfolio managers, data scientists, and leaders in our proprietary property management platform to build and execute investment and operational strategies that create value for clients. This role operates within a “start-up” environment within the Residential Opportunities platform, requiring comfort operating in a fast-moving, high-ambiguity environment where priorities shift and many problems will not have a predefined path to solution.




Responsibilities:




Data Analysis Expertise: Analyze structured and unstructured data related to residential real estate, property management, and credit underwriting operations to uncover and display insights that inform investment decisions.
UI and Presentation Design: Create and support data visualizations via PowerPoint, Python, Tableau, PowerBI.
Strategic Problem Translation: Translate business scenarios and investment ideas into succinct data problems by identifying and analyzing key drivers. Seamlessly align business objectives with appropriate cost functions, demonstrating an acute understanding of available data nuances.
End-to-End Project Ownership: Manage the end-to-end delivery of success criteria, data, algorithms, statistical models, and reporting tools. You will be expected to engage from project inception and work with relevant parties to collect, analyze, and output analysis / models for broad consumption. Work collaboratively within cross-functional teams to ensure timely and high-quality deliverables. Your adeptness will ensure efficient problem-solving and the realization of desired outcomes.
Financial Acumen Development: Demonstrate a proactive approach to expanding knowledge in investing and real estate fundamentals, leveraging analytical skills to explore market trends and financial concepts.
IT Stack Optimization: Comfortable setting up and troubleshooting data connections (e.g., Snowflake, APIs, MCPs) and partnering with IT teams to onboard new tools and systems.
Team Synergy: Collaborate seamlessly with fellow team members, lending your skills to group projects and promoting a cohesive and productive team dynamic.
Flexibility and Initiative: Thrive in a high-ambiguity environment where many assignments will be loosely defined, requiring you to independently frame the problem, identify the right approach, and drive to a conclusion without waiting to be directed. Intellectual curiosity and the habit of proactively seeking out answers on your own are essential to success in this role. Interest in AI and exploration of new analytical tools is a strong plus.




Qualifications:

1-3 years of work experience with a degree in Engineering, Computer Science, Information Systems, Data Science, Mathematics, or a related quantitative field; candidates with a business, finance, or economics background and strong demonstrated technical skills are also encouraged to apply
Experience with Python, SQL, Git
Experience with Azure, Snowflake, PowerBI, Fabric a plus. Otherwise, can learn in the role
Demonstrated ability to manipulate and summarize large datasets
Highly motivated with excellent attention to detail and organizational skills
Strong communication skills with the ability to present findings clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences, including senior leadership; comfortable translating ambiguous asks into structured analysis and working within a collaborative team environment




The base salary for this position is expected to be between $115,000.00 and $140,000.00. The base salary offered to the chosen candidate will be commensurate with a candidate’s relevant experience and other qualifications for the position, as determined by the Company in its sole discretion. In addition to base salary, this position is eligible for an annual discretionary bonus [which is often a meaningful portion of the compensation package], and a robust benefits package.

title: Financial Planning Analyst
description:
Our Story

J.Crew gets you dressed every day, for every occasion.

Since 1983, we’ve been creating classics that feel familiar and refreshingly new, crafted with unbeatable quality and distinctive point of view—it’s no wonder we’ve been in your closet for four decades and counting.




Today, we continue to do the classics our way, inspiring not only how you shop but how you define your personal style. Our commitment runs deeper than just making great clothes—we’re proud of our role in getting you dressed with confidence, character, and celebrating a distinctly American sensibility that makes us who we are.




Our dedication to Diversity, Equity, Belonging & Sustainability has been celebrated with industry recognition, reflecting our commitment to fostering an inclusive and sustainable workplace.




Overview:

The Analyst will be responsible for all aspects of financial planning, analysis, reporting, and ad hoc projects supporting the brand.




Responsibilities:

Develop comprehensive operating expense budgets & monthly forecasts for Stores and Ecommerce businesses
Establish and maintain cross functional business partnerships to drive financial objectives
Develop and implement meaningful, actionable and timely management reporting to inform business partners and senior leadership
Prepare monthly P&L reviews for senior leadership and provide commentary and business insights on performance
Collaborate with accounting and business partners on investigating variances to ensure accurate reporting and forecasting
Identify and implement process improvements through automation and process standardization
Prepare daily/weekly sales plans in collaboration with Planning and Store Operations team
Create supporting schedules and presentations for Board of Directors, External Reporting and Executive leadership requests




Qualifications:

1+ years in banking, consulting or financial planning and analysis or related experience
Role is onsite two days a week
Four-year college degree in accounting or finance
Strong work ethic with sharp attention to detail, variances and trends
Team player with exceptional communication, organizational and analytical skills
Ability to create strong working relationships with cross-functional teams
Agile learner, with the ability to adapt, pivot efficiently between tasks and manage multiple time-sensitive priorities simultaneously
Proficiency in Excel, PowerPoint. SAP and/or Hyperion experience a plus




We welcome you to apply, even if you don't check all the boxes. Our passion is scouting life-long learners who are driven by curiosity, and who feel connected to our brands and share our desire to make an impact. We’re always seeking bright new talent who leverage their unique experiences to discover, grow and evolve with our teams.




Benefits + Perks

Health & Well-being - Eligible associates and family members receive medical, dental, prescription and vision insurance, family planning (fertility, adoption & surrogacy support), fitness discounts, medical travel and more.
Associate Discount - We love our products just as much as you do! That’s why we offer a great associate discount across all of our brands (J.Crew, J.Crew Factory, and Madewell).
Summer Fridays - In addition to our hybrid work model, we also close our office at 1:00 PM every Friday during the Summer months.
Community Impact - We support the communities where we live and work through our philanthropic efforts and the J.Crew Cares Program.
Winter Break - In addition to our PTO package, J.Crew Group offers a winter break at the end of December to eligible full-time associates at the Home Office to provide time to refresh and recharge.




Note: Availability of these benefits and perks may be subject to work location & employment type and may have certain eligibility requirements.




At J.Crew Group (JCG) we aim to pay competitively for our company’s size and industry. The base salary offered will take into account internal equity and may vary depending on the candidate’s geographic region of work premises, job-related knowledge, skills, and experience among other factors. The base salary is just one component of J.Crew Group’s competitive total rewards strategy that also includes the opportunity for bonus, competitive benefits and perks.




One of our core focuses here at JCG is creating a community and culture that builds belonging. We are deeply committed to our Diversity, Equity and Inclusion efforts, and we warmly welcome job applicants of all backgrounds.




JCG is proud to affirmatively provide equal opportunity to all associates and qualified applicants without regard to race, color, religion, national origin or citizenship, age, sex, marital status, ancestry, legally protected physical or mental disability, veteran status, gender identity, sexual orientation or any other basis protected under applicable law.

title: Operations & Data Analytics Associate / Senior Associate
description:
As an Operations & Data Analytics Associate / Senior Associate, you will work at the heart of this ecosystem - using data to uncover inefficiencies, diagnose root causes, and fix real operational problems across the end-to-end ecommerce.

You will deep-dive into ambiguous issues, challenge assumptions, and design scalable, pragmatic solutions that can be implemented on the ground. Your work will directly influence how millions of parcels move across the region.




You will also partner with Shopee’s 8 markets to understand local nuances and operational constraints — adapting regional frameworks into scalable, practical solutions across diverse market environments.

If you are someone who enjoys figuring out why things break, gets energised by solving messy real-world problems, and wants to see your solutions drive tangible impact, this role is for you.




Analyse large, complex datasets to identify inefficiencies and performance gaps
Deep-dive operational issues to uncover root causes
Build analytical models, automation scripts, and dashboards to support decision-making
Propose and drive scalable, pragmatic solutions that can be executed across markets
Work closely with operations, product, and tech teams to turn insights into real-world fixes
Collaborate with regional and local stakeholders to adapt solutions based on market-specific realities
Measure impact post-implementation to ensure sustainable improvements




Requirements




Bachelor's degree in any discipline with a strong analytical component
Proficiency in Python and/or SQL
Strong analytical skills and comfort working with large, messy datasets
Innately curious and motivated to understand problems deeply
Structured thinker who can break down complexity into actionable steps
Self-driven, hands-on, and comfortable operating in ambiguity
Strong communication skills and ability to work across diverse stakeholders

title: Data Analyst
description:
Data Analyst

About Interchecks

Interchecks is a fast-growing B2B2C fintech payments company powering how money moves in highly regulated industries. Our platform enables enterprise customers to embed secure, compliant payment experiences directly into their products, helping them move, manage, and monetize payments at scale.

We partner with some of the most recognizable operators in fintech and online gaming, solving complex payment and compliance challenges with modern infrastructure and thoughtful product design. We care deeply about building reliable technology, moving fast without cutting corners, and creating a company where smart, motivated people can do the best work of their careers.

The Opportunity
As a Data Analyst, you'll be the primary owner for day-to-day descriptive & diagnostic analytics—turning team outputs into executive-ready insights and decks, answering ad-hoc questions, and enforcing consistent metrics and narratives while being a direct contact for key stakeholders. This position is ideal for someone who loves turning messy questions into clean answers and thrives at the intersection of technical analysis and business communication.

What You’ll Own

Monitor trends, quantify impact and deliver concise and compelling story
Create ad-hoc descriptive data summaries for a wide range of internal and external audiences
Document workflows, data definitions and processes to support compliance and organizational knowledge sharing

What Success Looks Like

Demonstrate a deep and ongoing understanding of key performance metrics and extract valuable insights while communicating them across all levels of an organization
Become the trusted analytical partner, build scalable dashboards that reduce manual reporting time, and establish best practices for data quality that the broader team follows

Need to Have

2-3 years in business intelligence, reporting, or data analysis
Proficient with Python based data analysis
Proficient in Tableau
Proficient in Excel and PowerPoint
Excellent written and verbal skills across all levels of an organization
Proficient in writing SQL queries to extract, transform, and analyze data from diverse data sources
Strong attention to detail and the ability to work with large datasets while maintaining data integrity
Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to interpret complex data sets and communicate insights effectively 
Strong data visualization design skills and modern presentation skills

Nice to Have

Strong understanding of data quality principles and best practices
Traits for Success at Interchecks

The people who thrive here tend to be:

Comfortable getting their hands dirty and operating without perfect information
Ownership-driven and accountable for outcomes, not just tasks
Thoughtful collaborators who bring clarity, not ego
Energized by building in fast-moving environments
Curious, pragmatic, and solutions-oriented
Compensation Transparency

The base salary range for this position is $75,000 to $130,000 annually. Final compensation will be determined within this range based on a number of factors, including but not limited to relevant experience, skills, role scope, and geographic location.

We are intentional about placing offers thoughtfully within the posted range to ensure internal equity, market alignment, and room for growth over time.

In addition to base pay, this role is eligible for:

Equity grants. You will be an owner of what you are building and share directly in the long-term success of Interchecks
Bonus Eligibility 
401(k) with company match
Medical, dental, and vision insurance
Unlimited paid time off and paid holidays
Wellness, learning, and home office stipends
Other benefits designed to support you both inside and outside of work

Final compensation decisions are made based on role scope, experience, and internal leveling.

Accessibility & Accommodations

Interchecks is committed to creating an inclusive and accessible hiring experience. If you require a reasonable accommodation to apply for a role or participate in the interview process, please contact peopleoperations@interchecks.com

Equal Opportunity Employer

Interchecks is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to building an inclusive environment for all employees. We do not discriminate based on race, color, religion, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, or any other legally protected status.

title: Trainee, Project Management
description:
Company Description

At Konecranes, we believe that great customer experience is built on the people behind the Konecranes name. Everything we do, we do with passion and drive. We believe diversity drives business success and is the foundation for our growth. We welcome different backgrounds and skills that enrich our community and we promote a place where we can ALL be ourselves. This is what makes Konecranes a unique place to work.

Job Description

Job Summary:

We are looking for a motivated and detail-oriented Trainee, Project Management to join our team. As a Project Management Trainee, you will assist in planning, executing, and closing projects under the guidance of senior project managers. This is an excellent opportunity to gain hands-on experience in managing real-world projects across various domains.

Key Responsibilities:


Organizing Kick off meeting for all assigned new order with Internal stake Holders for smooth order transfer.
Preparation and control of schedules/project plans for all orders.
Conduct regular planning & review meetings to ensure project timelines.

SAP Knowledge would be an extra advantage.

Awareness of ISO 14001 & 45001 Standards


Qualifications

Diploma (DET) – Electrical/Mechanical.

Excellent written and verbal communication
Proficiency in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint); familiarity with tools like SAP & MS Project is a plus.
Ability to work with team to achieve goals.
Target Driven
Eagerness to learn and grow in a fast-paced environment


Additional Information

What we offer you:

In return, we can offer you exciting and challenging Specialist – Project Management and flexible working environment to form a part of the Konecranes & Demag Industrial Cranes India team, you will create value added support locally and in delivering services as per our Lifting People Strategy.

Please apply through www.konecranes.com/career

Konecranes moves what matters. We are a global leader in material handling solutions, serving a broad range of customers across multiple industries. We consistently set the industry benchmark, from everyday improvements to the breakthroughs at moments that matter most, because we know we can always find a safer, more productive and sustainable way. That's why, with 16 000+ professionals in over 50 countries, we are trusted every day to lift, handle and move what the world needs.

Konecranes is committed to ensuring that all employees and job applicants are treated fairly in an environment which is free from any form of discrimination.

title: Human Resources Intern
description:
Job Title: HR INTERN

About Us

“Capco, a Wipro company, is a global technology and management consulting firm. Awarded with Consultancy of the year in the British Bank Award and has been ranked Top 100 Best Companies for Women in India 2022 by Avtar & Seramount. With our presence across 32 cities across globe, we support 100+ clients across banking, financial and Energy sectors. We are recognized for our deep transformation execution and delivery.

WHY JOIN CAPCO?

You will work on engaging projects with the largest international and local banks, insurance companies, payment service providers and other key players in the industry. The projects that will transform the financial services industry.

MAKE AN IMPACT

Innovative thinking, delivery excellence and thought leadership to help our clients transform their business. Together with our clients and industry partners, we deliver disruptive work that is changing energy and financial services.

#BEYOURSELFATWORK

Capco has a tolerant, open culture that values diversity, inclusivity, and creativity.

CAREER ADVANCEMENT

With no forced hierarchy at Capco, everyone has the opportunity to grow as we grow, taking their career into their own hands.

DIVERSITY & INCLUSION

We believe that diversity of people and perspective gives us a competitive advantage.




We’re Hiring: HR Intern (Full-Time | Contract to Hire)

Are you a recent MBA (HR) graduate looking to kick-start your HR career with hands-on exposure to HR operations, documentation, and process excellence in a global environment? This opportunity is for you!

🔍 Role Overview

We are hiring one HR Intern to support our HR team in clean-up and validation of Employee Personnel Files (P-Files) across APAC regions ahead of an SAP migration, and to assist in revising, updating, and publishing HR Process SOPs.

This role is ideal for a detail-oriented fresher who enjoys working with documents, process improvements, and structured data.

📍 Location & Work Model

Location: EC, Bengaluru
Work Model: Hybrid – Office on Tuesday & Wednesday
Shift: 7:00 AM – 4:00 PM IST
Weekly Off: Saturday & Sunday

⏳ Contract Details

Type: Intern (Full-Time)
Duration: 3 Months (Contract-to-Hire opportunity)
Notice Period: Immediate

✅ Key Responsibilities

Clean, organize, and validate Employee Personnel Files (P-Files) for APAC employees
Ensure documents meet compliance and audit standards
Assist in reviewing, updating, and formatting HR Process SOPs
Support SOP version control and publishing
Coordinate with the Hiring Manager/Coach for documentation accuracy
Maintain strict confidentiality of employee data

🎓 Eligibility & Skills

MBA / PGDM in HR (Fresher preferred | 0–6 months experience)
Strong MS Office skills (Excel, Word, PowerPoint)
Excellent written and verbal communication
High attention to detail and process orientation
Willingness to work on documentation-heavy tasks




If you are keen to join us, you will be part of an organization that values your contributions, recognizes your potential, and provides ample opportunities for growth. For more information, visit www.capco.com. Follow us on Twitter, Facebook, LinkedIn, and YouTube.

title: CA Fresher - Finance Analyst
description:
Responsibilities

For CA freshers only

Strategy and Planning


Strategic Planning: Participate in the development of long-term financial strategies, plans and objectives that align with the organizations mission and goals.
Business Planning: Conduct analysis and provide recommendations on business opportunities, market trends and competition.
Financial Projections: Prepare financial projections and provide insights into the organizations future financial performance.
Resource Allocation: Analyze and evaluate the allocation of resources to ensure optimal utilization of resources to achieve strategic goals.
Performance Tracking: Develop and implement performance tracking mechanisms to monitor progress against strategic plans and objectives.
Budgeting: Participate in the development and implementation of budgeting processes that support the organization's strategic goals.
Risk Management: Conduct risk assessments and develop risk mitigation strategies to minimize the impact of potential risks on the organization's financial performance.
Business Intelligence: Gather, analyze and interpret business data to provide insights into the organizations performance and future trends.
Market Analysis: Conduct market research and analysis to identify market trends and provide insights into the competitive landscape.
Performance Management: Develop and implement performance management processes that support the organization's strategic objectives.


Budgeting and Forecasting


Preparation of Budget: Prepare and maintain budgeting templates, develop detailed budget plans and monitor budget performance regularly.
Forecasting: Prepare financial forecasts using historical data and market trends, and provide regular updates to management.
Cost Analysis: Analyze cost structures and trends to identify areas for cost reduction, and provide recommendations to management.
Revenue Forecasting: Analyze revenue data, prepare revenue projections and provide recommendations to management on revenue growth opportunities.
Scenario Planning: Develop various forecasting scenarios to evaluate potential outcomes and support decision-making.
Variance Analysis: Analyze variances between actual and budgeted results and provide recommendations for improvement.
Trend Analysis: Monitor industry trends, competitor trends and economic trends to provide insights and support decision-making.
Risk Management: Assess and identify financial risks associated with budget and forecast outcomes and provide recommendations for mitigating such risks.
Collaboration with Business Units: Work with business units to understand their operational plans and incorporate them into budget and forecast plans.


Financial Planning and Reporting


Prepare and analyze financial reports: Generate and review financial statements, income statements, balance sheets, cash flow statements and other reports to provide insights into the financial performance of the organization.
Financial Planning: Develop and implement financial plans, budgets and forecasts that align with the organization's goals and objectives.
Variance Analysis: Conduct variance analysis to identify areas of overspending or underspending and provide recommendations to management.
Performance Evaluation: Evaluate the financial performance of the organization and provide recommendations to improve financial results.
Reporting to Management: Provide regular updates to management on the financial performance of the organization and highlight key financial metrics.
Stakeholder Reporting: Prepare and distribute financial reports to stakeholders, including shareholders, banks, and regulatory agencies.
Budget Monitoring: Monitor the organization's budget and ensure that expenditures are aligned with the approved budget.
Financial Dashboards: Develop and maintain financial dashboards to provide real-time insights into the organization's financial performance.
Financial Trends Analysis: Analyze financial trends, both internally and in the market, to provide insights into the organization's financial position.
Forecasting: Develop and maintain financial forecasting models to predict future financial results and identify potential risks and opportunities.


Analytics


Dashboard Development: Develop and maintain dashboards, scorecards and other reporting tools to provide actionable insights on business performance.
Predictive Modelling: Use statistical analysis and machine learning algorithms to predict future trends, patterns and potential risks.
Big Data Analysis: Work with large datasets and leverage data analytics tools to uncover insights and improve decision-making.
Financial Forecasting: Use analytics techniques to generate accurate and timely financial forecasts.


Cost Optimization: Utilize analytics to identify cost-saving opportunities and improve the efficiency of business operations.


Customer Analysis: Analyze customer data to understand buying patterns, preferences and identify opportunities for growth.
Sales Analysis: Analyze sales data to understand market trends, customer behavior and identify opportunities for growth.
Supply Chain Analysis: Analyze supply chain data to identify bottlenecks, improve efficiency and reduce costs.
Performance Monitoring: Monitor key performance indicators and provide regular reports on business performance.
Data Visualization: Create data visualizations to communicate complex data insights in an easy-to-understand format.

title: GSA - Front Office
description:
Company Description

Strategically located in the heart of the IT corridor- Novotel Bengaluru Outer Ring Road brings to you different categories of beautiful rooms that are sleek and contemporary designed, full of modern day amenities suitable for your stay. In proximity to numerous IT parks and corporate hubs notably- Ecospace Business Park, Prestige Techpark, Cessna Business Park, Salarpuria Techparks- Novotel Bengaluru Outer Ring Road offers the perfect residence to travelers across the globe.

Nearby to corporate offices- JP Morgan, Accenture, Honeywell, Deloitte and many more along Sarjapur Road and Marathahalli, Novotel Bengaluru Outer Ring Road brings to you seamless benefits of hosting events and conferences in well-equipped meeting spaces. Bengaluru, besides the Silicon Valley of the country, is close to some of the most significant historical and leisure destinations in South India- Mysore, Coorg, Goa, Munnar, Ooty, Pondicherry to name a few among the long list of leisure destinations.

Job Description

A Front Office Associate typically handles administrative tasks and ensures smooth operations at the reception area of a business. Their responsibilities include:


Greeting Guests: Welcoming visitors and clients, ensuring a positive first impression.
Answering Calls: Managing incoming and outgoing calls professionally.
Scheduling: Booking appointments or reservations, and coordinating meeting rooms.

title: Data Analyst -Treasury ( Freshers)
description:
Job Title: Data Analyst (Treasury Mid office)

Department: Treasury – Debt & Lending Alliances

Location: Propelld Corporate Office, HSR Bangalore

Experience: Freshers

Job Type: Full-Time




About Propelld:

Propelld is a leading fintech NBFC focused on providing innovative financial solutions in the education sector. We are data-driven, using cutting-edge analytics to offer personalized loan products and services to our clients.




Job Summary:

We are seeking a skilled personnel for Treasury mid office / Data Analyst with 2-3 years of experience, strong proficiency in Excel, and a solid understanding of financial numbers and treasury operations. The ideal candidate will gather, analyze, and present data-driven insights to help optimize product offerings, streamline operations, and enhance the customer experience.




Key Responsibilities:

Perform data collection, cleaning, and analysis of large datasets from multiple sources, including loan performance, customer data, financial transactions, and treasury operations.

Utilize Excel for advanced data analysis, including pivot tables, VLOOKUPs, macros, and complex formulas to generate insights.

Build and maintain automated reports and interactive dashboards to track key financial metrics using Excel, Tableau.

Support decision-making processes by providing actionable insights and trends derived from data, particularly focusing on financial and treasury-related information.

Collaborate with product, marketing, and operations teams to improve business performance through data-backed recommendations.

Conduct A/B testing and statistical analysis to evaluate the impact of business experiments and initiatives.

Use SQL for querying databases to extract and manipulate data efficiently.

Ensure the accuracy, consistency, and quality of data through validation and best practices, with a specific focus on treasury and financial data.

Present findings to both technical and non-technical stakeholders, including senior management.




Skills & Qualifications:

Preferred is Bachelor’s degree in Mathematics, Statistics, Economics, Finance, Computer Science, or a related field.

Strong expertise in Excel for data analysis and reporting, including advanced functions like pivot tables, macros, and formula-based analysis, especially with financial and treasury data.

Experience with SQL for data extraction and manipulation.

Familiarity with data visualization tools such as Tableau, Power BI.

Analytical mindset with the ability to interpret complex datasets and provide actionable insights, particularly in financial contexts.

Experience with financial numbers and treasury operations is a primary requirement.

Strong attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy.

Excellent communication skills for presenting data-driven insights to cross-functional teams.




What We Offer:

A fast-paced work environment in a growing fintech company.

Competitive salary and comprehensive benefits.

Learning and growth opportunities within a collaborative, inclusive team culture.

description:
Position: Fresher- Recruitment Trainee

Locations: Bangalore

Job Type: Full Time

Work Type: Onsite

Shift Timing: Night Shifts

Timing: 6:30 PM to 3:30 AM IST (Monday to Friday)

Transportation: Both Pick and Drop




Overview




TekWissen Group is a workforce management provider throughout India and many other countries in the world.




Job Description:




We are hiring passionate and motivated fresh graduates who are eager to build a career in US IT Recruitment.
As a US IT Recruiter, you will be responsible for sourcing, screening, and submitting potential candidates for various IT positions with our clients in the United States.
You will work closely with Account Managers and delivery leads to understand client requirements and ensure timely and quality submissions.
This is a full-time onsite position designed to train and mold freshers into skilled recruitment professionals in the highly dynamic US staffing industry.

Responsibilities:

Understanding Client Requirements (IT or Non-IT)
Regularly interact with Account Managers and Team Leads to get complete clarity on job requirements.
Understand the technical and functional aspects of each job role (skills, experience level, domain, and location).
Prioritize open requisitions based on urgency and importance.

Candidate Sourcing & Screening:

Use various sourcing channels such as job portals (Dice, Monster, CareerBuilder, etc.), LinkedIn, internal databases, networking, and referrals.
Perform keyword searches and Boolean searches to identify suitable profiles.
Screen candidates through phone/video interviews to evaluate communication skills, technical capabilities, availability, and salary expectations.
Ensure a high level of candidate quality before submission.

Resume Submission & Coordination:

Format and present resumes professionally before sharing with the Account Manager or client.
Maintain a quick turnaround time for candidate submissions to meet tight deadlines.
Follow up regularly with candidates to ensure their availability and interest.
Coordinate interviews between candidates and clients; track feedback and results.

Recruitment Tools & Documentation:

Maintain accurate records of candidate details, job status, and communication history using Applicant Tracking Systems (ATS).
Update internal databases and track candidate progress throughout the recruitment lifecycle.

Required Skills & Qualifications:

Education: Bachelor's degree (Any discipline).
Excellent communication skills is a must (spoken and written English).
Strong interpersonal skills, positive attitude, and willingness to learn.
Ability to multitask, prioritize, and work in a fast-paced environment and Proficient in using MS Office tools.




TekWissen Group is an equal opportunity employer supporting workforce diversity.

We give a chance to every talented and motivated student/ fresher intern to develop AI models from scratch within a 2-month timeframe and show case their talents. Design and implement complete AI models following provided specifications.
Develop both frontend and backend components of the platform.
Create and integrate AI modules.

title: Bolsista Graduado - Inova Talentos - Bosch -Desenvolvimento de conceito de remanufatura para componentes de reposição diesel
description:
O Programa Inova Talentos tem como foco selecionar, acompanhar e desenvolver bolsistas, além de coordenar ações com organizações parceiras. Ele convida indústrias a proporem projetos de Pesquisa, Desenvolvimento e Inovação (PD&I) nos quais a participação dos bolsistas promova o crescimento profissional em PD&I empresarial e contribua para a inovação da empresa. O programa oferece bolsas para graduandos, graduados, mestres e doutores, permitindo atividades de PD&I nas instalações das empresas pelo território nacional, após aprovação da Comissão Interna do IEL Paraná.

Projeto: Desenvolvimento de conceito de remanufatura para componentes de reposição diesel.




Sobre a Oportunidade:




A Bosch Curitiba está em busca de um profissional para atuar em um projeto estratégico de inovação no setor automotivo, com foco no desenvolvimento de soluções pioneiras em remanufatura. O profissional selecionado fará parte de uma iniciativa que envolve a análise aprofundada de componentes, aplicação de know-how avançado e criação de novos designs e processos produtivos com alto valor tecnológico e relevância de mercado. Trata-se de uma oportunidade única para integrar um ambiente de excelência, contribuir diretamente com a estratégia global da empresa no mercado de reposição e atuar na vanguarda da engenharia aplicada.




Formação Acadêmica:




Graduação em Engenharias; Mecânica, Mecatrônica, Elétrica, Eletrônica, automotiva ou áreas afins.

Idioma: Inglês - Nível Intermediário




Principais Atividades:

Estudar detalhadamente o design dos injetores, identificando características construtivas, materiais empregados e possíveis pontos críticos de falha e desgaste.
Desenvolver um conceito de manufatura do produto vigente, buscando identificar possíveis oportunidades de market-sharing no mercado.
Desenvolvimento de design de produto: Auxiliar no design de componentes, buscando maior robustez do produto.
Produzir amostras em pequena escala para validação inicial, com apoio dos setores de suporte da Bosch.




Conhecimento necessário:

Conhecimento em desing de produtos bem como manufatura (montagem e teste).
Conhecimento em testes/validação de produtos.




Conhecimento desejável e Soft Skills:

Autonomia, Autogestão, responsabilidade;

title: Internship or Thesis – Research & Development (m/f/d)
description:
Job Location

Kronberg

Job Description

Are you eager to lead exciting projects and have a meaningful impact? Dive into the magic behind our brands and take part in future innovations! If you're passionate about learning, eager to develop your skills, and driven to contribute to groundbreaking innovations, this internship is for you.

Join us and you could soon leave your mark in areas like natural sciences, materials, automation, sustainability, analytics through your project.

We offer both mandatory and voluntary internships as well as Bachelor and Master thesis opportunities at our German Innovation Center. We are closely connected as one European R&D community, and you can discover similar internship options at our other European Technical Innovation Centers.

YOUR ROLE

Here are some examples of projects we can usually offer:

Electrical or Mechanical or Packaging Engineering


Design of innovative mechanical & electronic concepts for smart physical prototypes
Drive for rapid prototyping through additive manufacturing technologies
Development of appealing packaging concepts with the best possible environmental footprint


Chemical or Physical Science


Lead a feasibility study for the development of a new raw material
Synthesis and characterization of super absorbing polymers for hygiene products
Formulation of hair care products and tests for various fragrance options


Natural-/Life Sciences/QA


Statistical analysis and interpretation of datasets to gain new consumer insights
Demo development to communicate benefit of new product to consumer
Test method development and validation to measure product or material performance


Computational engineering or Modelling and Simulation


Simulation and modeling of complex fluids and materials to predict product or process performance
Digitalization of manufacturing and supply chain processes
Development of coaching algorithms and gamification concepts for personalized consumer experience


Job Qualifications

YOUR PROFILE


You are a highly motivated and curious bachelor’s or master’s student and have already completed at least the first two semesters of your bachelor’s
You are enrolled for the full period of your internship and study at a European university with one of the following subjects:
Engineering: Mechanical-, Electrical-, Chemical-, Industrial-, Precision-Engineering, Mechatronic, Packaging, Computational, Mathematical or equivalent
Physical Sciences: Chemistry, Polymer Chemistry, Physics, Materials Technology and Science and Mathematical Modeling & Simulation
Natural-/Life Sciences such as Chemistry, Biology, Biochemistry, Biotechnology, Bio Physics, Statistics, or similar.
You are available for min. 3 and up to 12 months for an internship with a possible start every 1st of the month, for a thesis you should plan with 6 months duration
You have a track record of academic excellence and solid technical skills
You work effectively with diverse groups of people by embracing creativity, innovation, curiosity and initiative.
You demonstrate outstanding analytical and problem-solving skills, a drive for leadership, and a strong passion for technical challenges
You can communicate proficiently in English (written and spoken)


WHAT WE OFFER


Exciting work and responsibility from Day 1.
A competitive internship salary (monthly starting salary 2,300€).
Wide range of social benefits: e.g., flexible work environment, regular intern social connects, a buddy program, campus services such as fitness center and canteen etc.
A customized personal development plan including relevant coaching, mentorship, training-on-the-job, and a large portfolio of individual upskilling opportunities.
The experience to be an integral part of our multi-functional teams of passionate and inspiring people with flat hierarchies to get to know the unique P&G culture.
The chance to receive a full-time employment offer to start after your studies with diverse career perspectives including new job assignments every 2-3 years to gain experience in different areas and the opportunity to work abroad.
Opportunity to engage in our various employee programs: WeBelong Team & New Hire Network activities, GABLE Network & other affinity groups, Inspirational Leadership Events, Sports groups for running & football etc.
Collaborate & manage multi-functional teams with international spirit, work with colleagues across regions and all levels.
The option to combine your internship with your Bachelor / Master Thesis.


APPLICATION

To optimally prepare for our online assessment & interviews, make sure you fully understand our application process.


CV in English
Copy of university diploma (if applicable) and transcript
Copy of relevant work, internship and volunteering certificates or reference letters


Please upload all documents in the "CV/Resume" field. Incomplete applications may lead to delays in the recruitment process.

At P&G #weseeequal

We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. At P&G, we strive to build a culture where everyone feels welcome, included, and able to bring their full selves to work.

We ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process. Please click here if you require an accommodation during the application process and please make sure to wait to hear back from us regarding your accommodation before proceeding with the online assessment. We thank you in advance for your patience.

Job Schedule

Full time

Job Number

R000139944

Job Segmentation

Internships
Show more

title: Investment Banking Graduate Analyst - Singapore
description:
About Jefferies




Jefferies is a leading global, full-service investment banking and capital markets firm that provides advisory, sales and trading, research and wealth and asset management services. With more than 47 offices in 21 countries around the world, we offer insights and expertise to investors, companies and governments.




Our talented professionals provide a full range of advisory and underwriting services through creative and idea-driven M&A, leveraged finance, equity & equity-linked financing and restructuring & recapitalization solutions. With approximately 1,300 investment banking professionals we provide global coverage across all products and sectors.




What you can expect:




Jefferies Investment Banking Analysts have the unique opportunity to gain insight into a global, client-focused investment bank and experience an entrepreneurial environment where employees have the opportunity to make an immediate impact and are rewarded for performance. Our Analysts are exposed to Jefferies’ full range of top-tier investment banking products and leading industry expertise by working closely with senior bankers on pitches and live deals. Our lean deal team structure will not only provide you with unparalleled hands-on experience, it will help you develop lasting personal and professional relationships with peers and senior bankers that will serve as the foundation for a successful career.




All Investment Banking Analysts at Jefferies receive an enriching orientation comprising a structured training curriculum in August, once our entire 2026 Graduate Analyst cohort has joined us. Following an enriching orientation and training program, Analysts will begin working on their day-to-day responsibilities, with support from colleagues serving as both instructors and mentors. Jefferies’ collegial program also offers a series of social and educational events to further enhance your professional development and grow your network.




In addition to performing fundamental analysis and research into companies and markets, Analysts are challenged to think creatively, offer their input, and add value to the team from day one. As with everyone at Jefferies, Analysts are expected to pursue their projects with excellence, a collaborative spirit, and with the highest levels of integrity.




Analyst responsibilities may include, but are not limited to:

Performing financial valuation, discounted cash flow and multiples-based analyses
Building and using financial models
Preparing pitchbooks and live deal materials, such as offering memorandums and prospectuses
Conducting industry and product research
Analyzing detailed corporate and financial information
Participating in the execution of financings and M&A transactions




What we look for in a candidate

We look for candidates who are eager to make an impact by doing real, hands-on work and participating on active deal teams. Successful Jefferies Analysts are able to multitask and manage a variety of projects simultaneously. Candidates must demonstrate professionalism and client management skills together with strong technical, written and verbal communication skills. The work is fast paced, challenging and intellectually stimulating. The ability to work under pressure and tight deadlines, be adaptable and work both independently and as part of a team are essential traits of a successful candidate.




Although a background in accounting or finance can be helpful, we hire Analysts who have studied a variety of disciplines. A few more application particulars are enclosed below:




Candidates applying to this position must possess the right to work in Singapore (i.e.: is a Singaporean Permanent Resident or a Singaporean citizen).
Candidates who are fluent in a Southeast Asian language (e.g.: Bahasa Melayu, Bahasa Indonesia, Tagalog, Thai, Vietnamese, etc.) are encouraged to apply.
Candidates who have graduated / are graduating between October 2025 - March 2026 are eligible to apply.




Investment Banking

The Jefferies Singapore Investment Banking team focuses on the origination and execution of equity and M&A transactions for our Southeast Asian clients. Our business in Southeast Asia and Asia has a proven track record with tremendous deal momentum, which we continue to leverage to play an active role in M&A, initial public offerings and equity fundraises, cross-border financing across different sectors in the region. Our Investment Banking team works closely with teams in New York, London and other regional offices as one global team in cross-border transactions. Leveraging our unparalleled global resources and network, Jefferies’ Singapore platform is well positioned to capitalize on the continued growth of Southeast Asia and Asia economies.




Community & Engagement, Reasonable Accommodations and COVID




At Jefferies, we have made a commitment to build a culture that provides opportunities for all employees regardless of our differences, and supporting a workforce that is reflective of the communities where we work and live. As a result, we are able to pool our collective insights and intelligence to provide fresh and innovative thinking for our clients.




Jefferies is an equal employment opportunity workplace. We practice equal opportunity for all, regardless of race, religion, ancestry, color, gender, pregnancy, age, physical or mental disability or medical condition, national origin, marital, family and social status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, genetic information, religion, trade union membership or any other factor protected by law. We are committed to hiring the most qualified applicants and complying with all federal, state, and local equal employment opportunity laws. In accordance with applicable law, we are dedicated to finding reasonable accommodations for candidates and employees’ religious practices and beliefs, as well as any mental health or physical disability needs. Please inform your recruiter should you require accommodations throughout your application process.

title: TH Business Development (Warehouse) - Fresh Graduate
description:
About The Team

Why Join SCommerce BD?

In this role, you aren’t just an employee—you are a business consultant for our sellers. We have many different teams and we want to meet you first to see which area best fits your unique talents!

Job Description

What You Will Get to Do:

As a BD Associate, you will be the "bridge" between Shopee and our partners. Your potential scope includes;:


Grow Big Brands: Manage a portfolio of sellers (including famous Supermarket brands). You will advise them on strategy, negotiate deals, and help them increase their sales.
Hunt for New Partners: Identify and reach out to exciting new sellers. You will pitch Shopee’s services (like our fulfillment and retail models) and bring them onto our platform.
End-to-End Project Management: Take charge of the whole process—from creating professional pitch decks and negotiating rates to helping sellers register their shops and upload products.
Master the Numbers: Learn how to manage a "Profit & Loss" (P&L) statement. You will use data to track performance and find new ways for sellers to grow.
Relationship Building: Work closely with business owners to solve problems and build long-term success.


Requirements

Who We Are Looking For


Fresh Graduates: You have a degree in any field and are hungry to start your career in a fast-paced tech company.
Strong Communicators: You can speak and write clearly. You aren't afraid to pick up the phone or meet new people to pitch an idea.
Analytical Thinkers: You like working with data (Excel) to understand "the why" behind the numbers.
Fast Learners: Things change quickly here! You need to be able to adapt and learn new systems in a heartbeat.
The "Can-Do" Spirit: You are proactive, organized, and love hitting targets.


Why You’ll Love Working Here


Fast-Track Growth: You will learn more in one year at Shopee than in three years elsewhere.
Impact: See your hard work result in real sales and growth on the Shopee app.
Mentorship: Be guided by experienced leaders in the Thai e-commerce industry.

title: TH Business Development Project - Fresh Graduate
description:
About The Team:

The Shopee Business Development Project serves as a strategic driver of Shopee’s business expansion. The team combines market intelligence with hands-on execution to scale the platform through targeted seller education and commercial initiatives. As part of the Business Development Project, you will work closely with cross-functional teams to identify growth opportunities, support seller success, and ensure strategic projects are executed effectively across the organization. This role offers strong exposure to data, operations, and communications, making it an ideal launchpad for building a long-term career in E-commerce.

Job Description:


STRATEGIC PLANNING & BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE Roles in this track focus on commercial analysis, performance optimization, and project management. Responsibilities include competitive intelligence, P&L analysis, KPI development, budget management, and the design and execution of strategic initiatives to drive platform competitiveness and seller performance.


Representative Functions Include:


Competitive analysis and market intelligence
Pricing strategy and platform performance monitoring
Seller performance management and KPI governance
Revenue and asset monetization planning
Program and project management across seller initiatives
Cross-border business strategy and cluster management
Campaign performance analysis and optimization


Required competencies: Analytical thinking, business acumen, strategic problem-solving, stakeholder management, proficiency in quantitative analysis.


OPERATIONS & EXECUTION Roles in this track ensure the daily execution of business processes, administrative coordination, and operational support across seller-facing functions. Responsibilities include data management, quality assurance, process execution, and coordination of operational workflows.


Representative Functions Include:


Pricing operations and SKU matching
Quality assurance and performance monitoring
Revenue program operations (cashback, shipping, seller monetization)
Lead generation and data management
Seller acquisition and onboarding support
Campaign setup and execution
Administrative support and ticket resolution


Required competencies: Attention to detail, process discipline, efficiency in task management, ability to work under structured workflows, strong organizational skills.


MARKET DEVELOPMENT & COMMUNICATIONS Roles in this track drive seller acquisition, marketing solutions adoption, and community engagement. Responsibilities include managing seller advertising services, developing content strategies, conducting training, and executing brand partnership initiatives.


Representative Functions Include:


Shopee Ads service management
Livestream and video adoption programs
Seller communications and content management
Community engagement and event management
Brand and seller acquisition
Marketing solutions consulting (e.g., Facebook CPAS, Google Ads, affiliate programs)
Lead Generation & Acquisition Roles


Required competencies: Relationship building, communication skills, creativity, client service orientation, ability to influence and educate stakeholders.

Requirements:


Bachelor's degree in any discipline (graduated within the last 2 years or graduating in 2025)
Strong analytical and data interpretation skills with proficiency in Microsoft Excel (VLOOKUP, Pivot Tables, data manipulation, reporting)
Excellent communication skills in both Thai and English (written and verbal) for stakeholder management and cross-functional collaboration
Demonstrated problem-solving ability and process improvement mindset
High attention to detail with ability to manage multiple tasks and deadlines
Strong sense of ownership and accountability in executing deliverables
Fast learner with adaptability to changing business priorities and new tools
Ability to work both independently and collaboratively in cross-functional teams

title: TH Corporate Affairs - Fresh Graduate
description:
Job Description

The following are the core responsibilities for this role; you will also be expected to support the Corporate Affairs team on various projects and initiatives, and your duties will not be limited to this list.

Policy Research & Analysis Support


Regulatory Tracking: Monitor, track, and summarize daily/weekly updates on key legislative and regulatory developments in Thailand relevant to e-commerce, digital economy, and logistics (e.g., data privacy laws, new consumer protection guidelines).
Research Assistance: Conduct background research and gather data to support the team's policy positions.
Documentation: You might get the opportunity to assist in preparing drafts of policy submissions and internal policy briefings for management review.


Stakeholder Engagement & Coordination


Logistical Support: Provide essential administrative and logistical support for meetings, workshops, and events involving government officials, industry bodies, and external partners.
Database Management: Maintain and update the Corporate Affairs team's stakeholder contact list and engagement tracker with accuracy and diligence.
External Exposure: You might get the opportunity to attend external meetings with senior team members to observe policy discussions and learn effective engagement techniques.


Communications & Media Support


Media Monitoring: Monitor traditional and social media for relevant company mentions, industry news, and potential reputation issues.
Materials Preparation: Assist in collating data, proofreading, and formatting external communication materials, such as press releases, talking points, and presentations.
Crisis Support: You might get the opportunity to assist in preparing background materials during reputation or crisis management exercises.


Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) Execution


Project Implementation: Support the day-to-day execution and logistics of local CSR initiatives and community engagement projects.
Reporting: Assist in gathering success metrics, photos, and materials needed for internal and external CSR reporting.


Requirements


Education: Bachelor’s degree (Fresh Graduate) in Political Science, Public Administration, Law, Communications, International Relations, or a related discipline.
Experience: Relevant internship experience in government, public relations, public policy, or a technology company is highly desirable but not mandatory.
Knowledge: Demonstrable interest and foundational understanding of Thailand's regulatory and media landscape.
Communication: Excellent written and verbal communication skills in both Thai and English are required.
Skills:
Strong organizational skills and meticulous attention to detail.
Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, PowerPoint, Excel).
Ability to work effectively in a fast-paced, high-pressure environment.
A proactive attitude and a willingness to learn quickly from senior team members.

title: Management Trainee
description:
Overview:

We are seeking driven and talented individuals to join our Management Trainee Programme. This 1-year program is designed to cultivate leadership potential by providing intensive training, hands-on experience, and in-depth knowledge in a specific department. As a Management Trainee, you will be immersed in one functional area, learning the intricacies of the role, contributing to key projects, and building a solid foundation for a future leadership position within the organization.




Key Responsibilities:

Participate in comprehensive training and development programs to gain a thorough understanding of the chosen department (e.g., Engineering (Automotive, Mechanical, Mechatronic, Electrical, Computer, and Industrial engineering, Operations, Marketing, Sales, Finance etc.).
Assist in executing key initiatives and projects within the department to drive business goals and operational improvements.
Analyse department processes, provide insights, and suggest improvements to enhance efficiency and performance.
Collaborate with team members to ensure the successful implementation of business strategies and initiatives.
Prepare reports, presentations, and documentation to support decision-making processes.
Engage with senior leadership and department heads to gain mentorship, guidance, and career insights.
Undertake responsibilities progressively, moving toward independent management of tasks and small projects.
Participate in performance reviews and feedback sessions to monitor progress and set development goals.




Qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field (e.g., Engineering, Business, Finance, Marketing, HR, etc.).
CGPA 3.3 and above or equivalent
Fresh graduates or candidates with less than 1 year of work experience.
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and critical thinking skills.
Eagerness to learn and adapt to new challenges.
High level of initiative, drive, and resilience.
Ability to work well within a team and independently.
Interest in pursuing a long-term career in management within a specific department.




Program Benefits:

Tailored learning and development program focused on building expertise in a single department.
Close mentorship from experienced professionals and senior leaders.
Hands-on experience with real-world projects and business challenges.
Opportunities for career progression into management roles upon successful completion of the program.
Exposure to strategic decision-making processes within the department.




Interested candidates are invited to apply by pressing the Apply Button.

title: Graduate Trainee, Business & Corporate Banking (Small Medium Enterprises) - Fresh Graduates (Klang Valley)
location: Taman Wilayah, Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia
description:
If you are looking to excel and make a difference, take a closer look at us…

Overview

Our Graduate Trainee (GT) programs are developed for specific business divisions and are uniquely tailored in collaboration with business leaders. The GT programs serve to develop subject matter experts within their selected divisions, with the aim of creating a holistic young banker at the end of each program. We are currently hiring Graduate Trainees for Business & Corporate Banking in Small Medium Enterprises department. This is a sales role position; join a fast-paced environment where you don't just sell, you architect financial success for businesses, directly influencing the local economy.

Program Framework

GT Programs typically consist of 3 rotations across various departments within the division, supported by coaching from line managers. The first rotation includes on-boarding, attachment to a department and training including professional certifications. Training continues in the second and third rotation, together with any further exams for certification, during which there will be further opportunities to widen your depth of knowledge in your chosen program.

Other factors for consideration during interview process include examples of


Driving for results
Consistency in performance
Seeking win-win relationships
Demonstrating courage and humility
Taking ownership and make things happen
Leadership demonstrated through extra-curricular activities


Requirements


Malaysian citizen
Fresh graduates with a minimum CGPA 3.2 or 2nd Class Upper and its equivalent
Not more than 1 year working experience


For more job opportunities, please go to HLB Careers: https://hlb.wd3.myworkdayjobs.com/HLBCareers/

We appreciate your application and will be in touch with shortlisted candidates regarding next steps.

About Hong Leong Bank

We are a leading financial institution in Malaysia backed by a century of entrepreneurial heritage. title: Finance Graduate
description:
India, Hyderabad

Finance Graduate

Location: Hyderabad

Today, Lonza is a global leader in life sciences.

We design and deliver solutions that enable scientists to accelerate innovation and improve health outcomes. We are seeking a Finance Graduate to join our corporate finance team. This role provides exposure to financial planning, reporting, controlling activities, and evaluation of investment decisions. Graduates will interact closely with colleagues across Lonza’s global organization to address business challenges and opportunities, supported by a bespoke personal development plan and dedicated mentorship program.

Job Summary

The Finance Graduate program offers a 24‑month rotation across four different roles within Lonza’s Finance department, spanning local and global functions. This rotational program requires geographical mobility and provides a comprehensive learning experience, equipping graduates with the skills to contribute to Lonza’s financial strategy and operations.

Key Responsibilities


Provide analysis and support for financial reports and business plans.
Assist in presenting financial performance, forecasts, and budget variances, including identifying opportunities and risks.
Support analysis of financial information to detect errors and trends.
Contribute to projects and cost center controlling.
Assist with procurement processes such as requisitions and purchase orders.
Understand business cost drivers and provide insights to non‑finance stakeholders.
Support year‑end and internal audits.
Contribute to broader FP&A projects and continuous improvement initiatives.
Assist in preparation and analysis of SAR consolidation (Sales, Marketing, Admin, R&D).


Key Requirements

Education & Experience


Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Business Administration, Economics, or related field.
Strong academic background with interest in corporate finance.
Prior internship or experience in finance is an advantage.


Skills & Attributes


Strong analytical and problem‑solving abilities.
Effective communication and interpersonal skills.
Ability to work in a fast‑paced, global environment.
Business fluent in English.
Proactive mindset with eagerness to learn and adapt.
Willingness to relocate during rotational assignments.


About Lonza

At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With more than 30 sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values—Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion, and Integrity—reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives. Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. Lonza is committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.

title: ERP Product Designer (Fresher / Entry-Level)
description:
Job Description

About the Role

We are looking for a highly driven and accountable Product Designer who can take ownership of the complete ERP design lifecycle.

This role is ideal for freshers or early-career candidates who have strong communication skills, logical thinking, and the ability to understand business processes and convert them into structured product workflows.

Key Responsibilities

Client Interaction & Demo


Conduct product demos and explain ERP capabilities to clients
Understand client business processes and requirements


Requirement Gathering & BRD


Create detailed Business Requirement Documents (BRD)
Ensure requirements are clearly documented and approved


Workflow Design


Create structured workflows and process flows
Compare existing systems with ERP solutions and identify gaps
Get client approval before moving to design stage


Wireframing


Design wireframes using Figma after workflow confirmation
Ensure alignment between workflows and UI


Client Communication


Explain workflows, system logic, and development process
Handle feedback and refine outputs


Development Coordination


Work closely with developers to ensure correct implementation
Provide clarity and ensure alignment with requirements


Testing & Delivery


Coordinate with testers to ensure a bug-free product
Participate in final delivery and client approval


What We’re Looking For


Strong communication and presentation skills
Good analytical and problem-solving ability
Ability to understand business workflows quickly
Basic knowledge of Figma (or willingness to learn fast)
High ownership, accountability, and responsibility mindset
Confidence to interact directly with clients


Selection Process (Important)

We follow a practical evaluation approach instead of traditional interviews:

5-Day Real-Time Assessment


Candidates will work on live, practical tasks simulating real project scenarios
Tasks will evaluate:
Requirement understanding
Communication
Workflow thinking
Problem-solving
Ownership & accountability

Final selection will be based on performance during these 5 days

Who Should Apply


Freshers ready to take real responsibility from Day 1
Candidates who prefer hands-on learning over theoretical interviews
Individuals confident in communication and structured thinking


Why Join GwayERP


Direct exposure to real client interactions
Opportunity to work on end-to-end product lifecycle
Fast learning environment with high ownership roles
Build strong foundation in ERP product design & business systems

title: Junior Engineer-Process Engineering
description:
Junior Engineer-Process Engineering Gradiant India Private Limited Chennai, India 4/1/26 Process Engineering

About The Organization

Gradiant is a Different Kind of Water Company. With a full suite of differentiated and proprietary end-to-end solutions for advanced water and wastewater treatment powered by the top minds in water, the company serves its clients’ mission-critical operations in the world’s essential industries, including semiconductors, pharmaceuticals, food & beverage, lithium and critical minerals, and renewable energy. Gradiant’s innovative solutions reduce water used and wastewater discharged, reclaim valuable resources, and renew wastewater into freshwater. The Boston-headquartered company was founded at MIT and has over 1,400 employees worldwide.

Discover us at gradiant.com.

Job Overview

The Jr. Process Engineer plays a key role in the design, development, and optimization of water and wastewater treatment plants. This position involves contributing to proposal preparation and executing process engineering activities aligned with project and client requirements. The role requires close collaboration with sales, business development, process engineering, and new product introduction teams to ensure timely and cost-effective project delivery. The Process Engineer ensures that all work complies with company standards and fosters a culture of continuous improvement.

Responsibilities


Develop and implement innovative water and wastewater treatment processes to enhance operational efficiency and effectiveness.
Design process trains for water and wastewater treatment plants in coordination with the process engineering team.
Perform equipment design and sizing to meet process requirements.
Prepare and review detailed Process Flow Diagrams (PFDs), Heat and Mass Balances, and Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams (P&IDs).
Coordinate with cross-functional teams including business development, engineering, project management, operations, and safety during proposal preparation.
Participate in vendor technical evaluations, risk assessments, and contribute to selection decisions.
Develop comprehensive project cost estimates throughout the project lifecycle and collaborate with business development for pricing.
Deliver client proposals punctually while maintaining high standards of technical quality.
Participate in and present during customer meetings, including bid clarifications and safety/risk workshops such as HAZOP, HAZID, and FMEA.
Prepare detailed process engineering deliverables including control logic, datasheets, commissioning and pre-commissioning procedures, supporting project and engineering teams post-award.
Assist with commissioning and site support activities as required.
Maintain clear communication with all project stakeholders, providing status updates and transparency on project progress.
Utilize membrane simulation tools, statistical analysis, and data reconciliation software for design calculations.
Continuously optimize treatment processes ensuring adherence to regulatory requirements and industry best practices.
Review and approve key project documents including design bases, design packages, and equipment specifications.
Provide technical training to internal teams and external customers on treatment processes and related technologies.
Keep abreast of emerging technologies, industry trends, and evolving regulations in water and wastewater treatment.
Evaluate existing treatment processes and recommend improvements
Develop and maintain Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) and training documentation for treatment processes.
Ensure compliance with all safety, health, and environmental regulations related to assigned activities.
Analyze customer RFQ, tender packages, and user requirement specifications to clearly identify and meet client needs
Prepare project kickoff packages and transfer detailed documentation to the execution teams.


Requirements

Qualification

Bachelor's degree, Post Graduate Diploma, or Professional Degree in Chemical or Environmental Engineering is highly desirable.

Skills


Familiarity with a variety of water and wastewater treatment technologies.
Good understanding of membrane filtration processes and membrane system design.
Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving skills.
Excellent communication and teamwork abilities.
Detail-oriented, reliable, and well-organized.
Proficiency in English (both verbal and written).
Willingness to travel as required.
Ability to work effectively in a fast-paced, high-energy, start-up environment.
Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to convey complex technical information clearly to team members and clients.
Customer-focused with the flexibility and responsiveness to meet diverse client demands.
Proactive and driven to challenge and improve existing processes and standards.
Rigorous attention to detail, ensuring accuracy and quality in all deliverables.
Ability to work independently with minimal supervision while contributing positively to team efforts.


Experience

Minimum 3 years of experience in process and application engineering within the water and wastewater sector.

Certificate

Relevant Certifications

Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO)

Gradiant is an equal opportunity employer. Qualified applicants will be considered without regard to race, color, religion, creed, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, national origin, age, veteran status, disability, or any other protected class.

title: Assurance Senior- Suriname
description:
For our office in Suriname we are looking for Assurance Seniors.

The Opportunity

Our Assurance group is one of the most trusted, respected and influential organizations in the industry. As part of our team, you will provide peace of mind to clients by using your skills and experience to give companies, investors and regulators confidence in the validity of financial statements and business-critical information or processes.

Join our Audit team and you will help our clients meet their reporting requirements by providing an objective and independent examination of their financial statements, timely and constructive discussion with management on accounting and reporting matters, and a robust and clear perspective to audit committees.

You will experience ongoing professional development through diverse experiences, world-class learning and individually tailored coaching. That’s how we develop outstanding leaders who team to deliver on our promises to all of our stakeholders, and in so doing, play a critical role in building a better working world for our people, for our clients and for our communities. Sound interesting? Well this is just the beginning. Because whenever you join, however long you stay, the exceptional EY experience lasts a lifetime.

Responsibilities, Qualifications, Certifications - External Responsibilities


Develop and maintain productive working relationships with client personnel and assess clients' satisfaction.
Proactively maintain contact with the client throughout the year.
Direct fieldwork daily, inform supervisors of the engagement status, and manage staff performance.
Adapt the audit approach to the changing client environment.
Demonstrate a thorough understanding of complex accounting and auditing concepts and apply them to client situations.
Develop people through effectively delegating tasks and providing guidance to staff.
Provide performance feedback and training, and conduct performance reviews.
Foster an efficient, innovative, and team-oriented work environment.
Contribute ideas/opinions to the audit teams and listen/respond to other team members' views.
Use technology to continually learn, share knowledge with team members, and enhance service delivery.
Maintain an educational program to continually develop skills.
Develop an understanding of EY's service lines and actively seek/encourage team members to contribute ideas and identify opportunities to apply the firm's services.


Responsibilities, Qualifications, Certifications - External

To qualify for this challenging and rewarding position that offers an excellent opportunity for career advancement, candidates must have:


An undergraduate or graduate degree in accounting or any other business related subject and/or an accounting designation i.e. ACCA, CA, CGA, CPA etc.
A minimum of 3 years of experience working as an auditor for a public accounting firm, or particular business/industry experience to meet special needs.
Excellent project management skills.
Advanced written and verbal communication skills (Dutch & English).
A dedication to teamwork and leadership.
Integrity within a professional environment.


What’s In It For You:


Accelerate your technical capabilities and transformative leadership skills with future-focused courses and development programs.
Broaden your horizons by working on highly integrated teams across the globe and collaborating with people from diverse backgrounds—both professionally and culturally.
Bring out the best in yourself with continuous investment in your personal well-being and career development.
Develop your own personal purpose and help us create a positive ripple effect on our teams, our business, clients, and society.


Are you ready to shape your future with confidence? Click to apply!
